title: Chapter 16: The Spider's Army
content: Count Orsi's coalition arrived on the eighty-fifth day of the truce.

Not with fangs or claws or the traditional terrors of a vampire assault. With *information*. A missive delivered by a thrall who collapsed and died on Greyhaven's outer gate, the message burned into his skin in letters of scar tissue. It was addressed to Kael, but it was meant for the world.

*Viscount Ashworth has violated the Veil Doctrine. Through a forbidden Blood Pact with a human paladin, he has achieved full daylight immunity. He has weaponized this immunity, using sunlight to assassinate vampires during their sacred rest. He has freed humans from lawful blood-bondage. He has turned volunteers into a private army that ignores every principle of the Hierarchy.*
*The Veil is broken. The secret is spreading.*
*I, Count Alessio Orsi, call upon every loyal vampire to unite against this threat. Any who shelter Ashworth share his guilt. Any who fight for him share his fate.*

The war room was silent when Kael finished reading. Sera's golden veins pulsed faster than usual — she was angry, he could feel it through the Pact, a cold fury that had nothing to do with fear. Marcus's silver eyes had gone hard. Even Baron Kestrel, who'd served the Rot King for fifty years, looked unsettled.

"He's burning the forest to catch one wolf," Marcus said. "If the Veil breaks, vampires lose everything. Every clan. Every Count. Every Prince. Human militaries mobilize. The Silver Dawn gets unlimited funding. It becomes an extinction-level event."
"He won't actually do it. The Veil protects him as much as us."

"He doesn't have to do it. The *threat* is enough." Marcus placed both hands on the map table. "Every vampire who reads this will panic. Panicked vampires do stupid things. We're about to have every major clan in the eastern territories knocking on our door."
"How many?" Kael asked.

"Our scouts count twelve Counts. Maybe forty Viscounts. Hundreds of Barons. Thousands of fledglings and thralls. Plus whatever human mercenaries Orsi's managed to buy with blood-tribute." Marcus traced the map with his finger. "They're massing here, at the Thorn Gate — the old border fortress between Greyhaven and the eastern territories. They'll hit us within the week."
"And our forces?"

"Polaris Guard: forty-three soldiers. All daylight-capable. All trained. Plus maybe two hundred Greyhaven militia — Barons and fledglings who swore fealty after Dredd died. They can't fight during the day, but they can hold the tunnels at night."

Kael studied the map. The Thorn Gate was a chokepoint — a narrow pass through the underground tunnels that connected Greyhaven to the eastern territories. Defensible, if you had enough soldiers. A death trap, if you didn't.
"Commander Voss," he said. "What's the Silver Dawn's position?"

Sera answered before anyone else could. "My father's forces are three days east. He's got two thousand soldiers. Paladins, scouts, heavy infantry. If Orsi's army is coming through the Thorn Gate, Voss can hit their rear echelon while they're focused on us."
"That's pinning them between two forces."
"It's also asking my father to ally openly with vampires. He'll lose half his officers if he does that."

"He'll lose more than officers if Orsi wins." Kael straightened. "Send word to Voss. Tell him the attack is coming from the Thorn Gate within the week. Tell him we'll hold the pass for as long as we can. And tell him..." He paused. "Tell him his daughter is still fighting."
The next seventy-two hours were a blur of preparation.

Kael mass-turned ten new recruits in a single night. The process was brutal — ten humans screaming through the transformation as their bodies rebuilt themselves into weapons. Ten new vampires who would share his daylight immunity through the Blood Pact link. Ten new soldiers for the Polaris Guard, bringing their numbers to fifty-three.

Marcus drilled them relentlessly. Formation tactics. Crossbow accuracy. The ten-second window between torpor and combat readiness that made daytime assaults possible. He ran them through simulated attacks in the Keep's training halls until they could strike a target in total darkness without hesitation.
"You're pushing them too hard," Sera observed on the third morning. She was standing at the edge of the training hall, her golden veins dim but still visible beneath her armor.

"I'm pushing them exactly as hard as they need to be pushed. The coalition isn't going to go easy on them." Marcus watched his soldiers run another drill. "Most of these people have never fought anything stronger than a fledgling. In three days, they're going to face Barons and Viscounts and maybe a Count. If they're not ready, they're dead."
"And if they burn out before the battle starts?"
"Then they wouldn't have survived the battle anyway." He turned to face her. "How are you holding up?"

Sera looked at her hands — at the golden veins, the faint luminescence, the skin that was slowly becoming something other than skin. "I'm changing faster. The closer we get to combat, the more the Pact... responds. It's like it knows. Like the bond is preparing for something."
"For what?"

"I don't know. But it feels bigger than a battle. Bigger than the Thorn Gate. It feels like..." She paused, searching for words. "Like the Pact is waiting for Kael to reach Prince rank. And until he does, it's going to keep pushing us both. Him toward power. Me toward... whatever I'm becoming."
Marcus was quiet for a moment. Then he said: "When this is over — when Orsi is dead and Greyhaven is safe — what happens to you?"

"I don't know. The texts say the transformation completes at Prince rank. But none of them say what the anchor becomes. Human. Vampire. Something else." She met his eyes. "I might not be me anymore. Or I might be more me than I've ever been. The only thing I'm sure of is that Kael will still be there. And that's enough."
"It's enough for you."

"It's enough for now." She turned back to the training hall, where fifty-three vampire soldiers were running their drills under the artificial light of Greyhaven's torches. "The rest we figure out as we go."
